rulings.Case Title: OLLIE ROBINSON JR VS. NODDIE BELL ROBINSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 6, 2013, Line 21, PLAINTIFF OLLIE ROBINSON's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. The motion is denied. Plaintiff unreasonably delayed filing this motion and allowing amendment one week before trial would prejudice defendant by inserting new claims and parties as to which there has been no opportunity for discovery, which is now closed. The order is without prejudice to renewing the motion if the trial is continued and discovery reopened. The court takes no position as to whether such relief would be appropriate. rulings.Case Title: GEORGIANA COOPER VS. BLOOMINGDALE'S INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bloomingdale'S Inc.'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 6, 2013, Line 23, Defendant Bloomingdale's Inc.'s Motion For Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is Granted in part and Denied in part. The motion for summary adjudication on the third cause of action for discrimination is granted. Defendant has shifted the burden by providing evidence that it had legitimate business reasons for its actions with respect to Plaintiff. Plaintiff has not provided substantial evidence tending to show that Defendant acted with a discriminatory motive based on her race. The most she can show is that she (and perhaps one other employee) felt there was discrimination, but she points to no concrete evidence supporting that belief such as racial slurs or comments or any of the other sorts of evidence on which other courts have relied. The motion for summary adjudication on the first cause of action for retaliation is denied. Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence to create a triable issue of material fact with respect to whether Defendant retaliated against Plaintiff for filing a claim with the EEOC. Although temporal proximity alone is not enough, according Plaintiff the benefit of all inferences a reasonable jury might draw, the closeness in time of Defendant's disciplinary action to Plaintiff's EEOC filing combined with Defendant waiting an uncharacteristic length of time to take action against Plaintiff and doing so only after the EEOC claim is sufficient to survive this motion. The motion for summary adjudication on the second cause of action for wrongful termination is denied for the same reason. The motion for summary adjudication on the issue of punitive damages is denied. Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence to create a triable issue of material fact with respect to whether Ms. Rock qualifies as a "managing agent." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: ALEJANDRO MAIRENA et al VS. GMG JANITORIAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 6, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANTs GMG JANITORIAL, INC. and GINA GREGORI's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue regarding unclean hands which, in any event, requires the trial court to weigh the evidence adduced at trial based on all the facts and circumstances in the case. Defendants' objections to plaintiffs' evidence concerning what they were told and instructed by Josefa Rodruiguez are overruled. Those statements are not offered for their truth but for the fact that the instructions were given. The alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendants failed to comply with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1350(b) by not stating each issue separately in the same words in the notice and separate statement or listing the specific material facts separately for each issue. In any event, there would be triable issues. rulings.Case Title: JUVENTINO ESTRADA VS. WELLS FARGO & COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Juventino Estrada'S Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 6, 2013, Line 2, part 1 of 2. Plaintiff Juventino Estrada's Motion to Compel Further Responses and Production of Documents Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JUVENTINO ESTRADA VS. WELLS FARGO & COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Juventino Estrada'S Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 6, 2013, Line 2, part 2 of 2. Plaintiff Juventino Estrada's Motion to Compel Further Responses and Production of Documents The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in Part and Denied in Part. March 22, 2013 was the last date for plaintiff to serve notice of a motion to compel. Paragraph 6 of the Ferrandini declaration rebuts the CCP Section 1013a(3) presumption of invalid service of the motion papers created by the fact that the mailing date stated in the McCray declaration is March 22, 2013 and the postage meter date on the envelope for the moving papers is March 25, 2013. Plaintiff has demonstrated an entitlement to discovery pursuant to the procedure set forth in Colonial Life & Accident Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1982) 31 Cal. 3d 785, but Plaintiff is not entitled to the identities of other claimants until they have consented to the release of their claims files. Also, given the evidence of 7,450 claims nationwide from December 1, 2008 to January 9, 2013, it is appropriate to limit discovery to claims from California residents whose claims were denied on the basis of an excluded event. National Union's counsel shall prepare and send Colonial Life requests for authorization to release medical records to California residents whose claims were denied on the basis of an excluded event between December 1, 2008 to January 9, 2013. The parties are to meet and confer to agree on appropriate wording of the letter and release. National Union shall produce the claims files for those claimants who authorize release, shall provide copies of the release authorizations to plaintiff, and once all such claims files have been produced shall serve a verified statement that all such files have been produced. In light of the foregoing, Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to interrogatories - set two is granted and denied as follows: Interrogatory Nos. 32 through 35 DENIED; Interrogatory Nos. 36 & 37 - GRANTED with respect to legal actions relating to the denial of a disability claim from January 1, 2008 to January 9, 2013; Interrogatory Nos. 38 through 40 - GRANTED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES R PRUTSMAN VS. RUST CONSULTING, INC., A MINNESOTA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Dismiss Or Stay First Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 6, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANTs SOUCEHOV HOLDINGS, INC. and RUST CONSULTING, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss Or Stay First Amended Complaint. The motion to dismiss based on the mandatory venue selection clause is granted. On May 16, 2011, Mr. Prutsman executed the Adoption Agreement, where he "hereby adopt[ed] the Stockholder Agreement with the same force and effect as if he were originally a party thereto." (Coen Decl., Exhibit 5.) The Stockholder Agreement provides a valid venue selection clause where the parties submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Delaware State Chancery Court. (Ellinkos Decl., Exhibit 4, § 10(e), p. 54.) A forum-selection clause is prima facie valid and will be enforced unless the resisting party meets the 'heavy burden' of proving enforcement would be unreasonable under the circumstances of the case. Mr. Prutsman has not met his heavy burden. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 6, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT 1ST FAMILY DENTAL OF CHICAGO, INC.'s (INCORRECTLY sued as First Family Dental) Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Off calendar. Dismissal of the entire action filed on April 19, 2013. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: TERRI TAFOLLA VS. CHRISTINE TOUR-SARKISSIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Petition To Compel Arb
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 6, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANTs CHRISTINE TOUR-SARKISSIAN and TOUR-SARKISSIAN LAW OFFICES' Petition To Compel Arbitration. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)
